title: The Chariot Metaphor
summary: Metaphor of the chariot: the body is the chariot, intellect is the driver, mind is the reins, senses are the horses, and the Self is the passenger. Addresses self-discipline, focus, overthinking, and study habits.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “The cave of the heart is occupied by two entities (the cosmic self and the individual self), enjoying the fruits of their actions. Those who are…” through “That person who has his (discriminative) intelligence as his charioteer and whose mind has been reined in , reaches the terminus of the journey,…” through “Whoever relates with devotion , this highly secret-laden episode in the assembly of the learned or at the time of performing rites and rituals…”

attribution.rights: Public domain in the United States

verses.sanskrit: ऋतं पिबन्तौ सुकृतस्य लोके गुहां प्रविष्टौ परमे परार्धे । छायातपौ ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति पञ्चाग्नयो ये च त्रिणाचिकेताः ॥
verses.translation: The cave of the heart is occupied by two entities (the cosmic self and the individual self), enjoying the fruits of their actions. Those who are realised, call them light and shade, as also the tenders of the five fires (house-holders), who had performed Nachiketa sacrifice thrice.

verses.sanskrit: यः सेतुरीजानानामक्षरं ब्रह्म यत् परम् । अभयं तितीर्षतां पारं नाचिकेतँ शकेमहि ॥
verses.translation: We can perform the Nachiketa sacrifice which will take us to the celestial regions, acting as a bridge. we could also attain the knowledge about the everlasting principle, Supreme Brahman, which is the place beyond the realm of fear and the refuge of the seekers of liberation - from human bondage.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मानँ रथितं विद्धि शरीरँ रथमेव तु । बुद्धिं तु सारथिं विद्धि मनः प्रग्रहमेव च ॥
verses.translation: The inner being is the controller (seated in) of the chariot, which is the body, the intellect the driver and the mind the reins.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि हयानाहुर्विषयाँ स्तेषु गोचरान् । आत्मेन्द्रियमनोयुक्तं भोक्तेत्याहुर्मनीषिणः ॥
verses.translation: They (the wise) say that the sense organs are the horses, the road is the sense objects and the experiencer is the body, sense organs and the mind, say the realised ones.

verses.sanskrit: यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्ययुक्तेन मनसा सदा । तस्येन्द्रियाण्यवश्यानि दुष्टाश्वा इव सारथेः ॥
verses.translation: That One whose understanding of the reality is not there and whose mind is without restraint, his sense organs behave without control like the rogue horses of a charioteer.

verses.sanskrit: यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति युक्तेन मनसा सदा । तस्येन्द्रियाणि वश्यानि सदश्वा इव सारथेः ॥
verses.translation: But he who has right understanding and whose mind is always restrained, his senses become disciplined like the obedient horses of a charioteer.

verses.sanskrit: यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्यमनस्कः सदाऽशुचिः । न स तत्पदमाप्नोति संसारं चाधिगच्छति ॥
verses.translation: Transmigratory (cyclic) existence is for him who has no control over his mind, lacks the right understanding and is always contaminated in mind, as he never attains that goal.

verses.sanskrit: यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति समनस्कः सदा शुचिः । स तु तत्पदमाप्नोति यस्माद्भूयो न जायते ॥
verses.translation: However, that one who is discriminating , has his mind under restraint and always pure (in thought), verily he attains that destination, from where he never reverts to re-birth.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञानसारथिर्यस्तु मनः प्रग्रहवान्नरः । सोऽध्वनः पारमाप्नोति तद्विष्णोः परमं पदम् ॥
verses.translation: That person who has his (discriminative) intelligence as his charioteer and whose mind has been reined in , reaches the terminus of the journey, which is that supreme abode of Lord Vishnu.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियेभ्यः परा ह्यर्था अर्थेभ्यश्च परं मनः । मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेरात्मा महान्परः ॥
verses.translation: The objects of senses are superior to the sense organs, the mind is superior to the sense objects, the intellect is greater than the mind and the great Atman is superior to the intellect .

verses.sanskrit: महतः परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः । पुरुषान्न परं किंचित्सा काष्ठा सा परा गतिः ॥
verses.translation: The Unmanifested is higher than the great ( Atman), and the Purusha higher than the Unmanifested. That is nothing superior to the Purusha, it is the acme and the supreme refuge.

verses.sanskrit: एष सर्वेषु भूतेषु गूढोऽऽत्मा न प्रकाशते । दृश्यते त्वग्र्यया बुद्ध्या सूक्ष्मया सूक्ष्मदर्शिभिः ॥
verses.translation: This Atman, is not seen, though laid secretly in all beings. However, it is seen by the Seers of the subtle , through their incisive and subtle power of their intellect.

verses.sanskrit: यच्छेद्वाङ्मनसी प्राज्ञस्तद्यच्छेज्ज्ञान आत्मनि । ज्ञानमात्मनि महति नियच्छेत्तद्यच्छेच्छान्त आत्मनि ॥
verses.translation: The wise should dissolve the speech in the mind, the mind in the intellect, the intellect in the Great Atman , and that Great Atman in the Atman of quietitude.

verses.sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत । क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति ॥
verses.translation: Arise ! Awake! Having resorted to an excellent preceptor , realise ( the Reality ) . The people with far-sight say that like the sharp edge of a razor, that path is both difficult to set foot upon and to cross.

verses.sanskrit: अशब्दमस्पर्शमरूपमव्ययं तथाऽरसं नित्यमगन्धवच्च यत् । अनाद्यनन्तं महतः परं ध्रुवं निचाय्य तन्मृत्युमुखात् प्रमुच्यते ॥
verses.translation: One gets rid of the cyclic existence of births and deaths on realising that ultimate Reality, which is devoid of sound, touch, form, perishability, taste, smell and which is constant, beyond the Mahat, does not undergo any change and has neither a beginning nor an end.

verses.sanskrit: नाचिकेतमुपाख्यानं मृत्युप्रोक्तँ सनातनम् । उक्त्वा श्रुत्वा च मेधावी ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥
verses.translation: Having listened attentively to the ancient episode of Nachiketa, as narrated and discussed by the god of Death , the Wise One revels in the world of Brahma and is glorified.

verses.sanskrit: य इमं परमं गुह्यं श्रावयेद् ब्रह्मसंसदि । प्रयतः श्राद्धकाले वा तदानन्त्याय कल्पते । तदानन्त्याय कल्पत इति ॥
verses.translation: Whoever relates with devotion , this highly secret-laden episode in the assembly of the learned or at the time of performing rites and rituals for the Infinitude , indeed becomes fit for the Infinitude.
